641: function toReviewFindingRecord(value: unknown): ReviewFinding[] {
642:   if (!Array.isArray(value)) {
643:     return [];
644:   }
645: 
646:   // Phase 2 contract: strict V2 query surfaces intentionally exclude legacy finding shapes.
647:   // We do not attempt semantic V1->V2 conversion at read time because legacy reports were
648:   // generated without full ReviewContext and are not trustworthy for canonical V2 semantics.
649:   return value.flatMap((item) => {
650:     const record = item && typeof item === 'object' && !Array.isArray(item) ? (item as Record<string, unknown>) : null;
651:     if (!record) {
652:       return [];
653:     }
654: 
655:     const severity = typeof record.severity === 'string' ? record.severity.trim() : '';
656:     const category = typeof record.category === 'string' ? record.category.trim() : '';
657:     const passType = typeof record.passType === 'string' ? record.passType.trim() : '';
658:     const description = typeof record.description === 'string' ? record.description.trim() : '';
659:     const suggestedFix = typeof record.suggestedFix === 'string' ? record.suggestedFix.trim() : '';
660:     if (!severity || !category || !passType || !description) {
661:       return [];
662:     }
663: 
664:     if (!['info', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'].includes(severity)) {
665:       return [];
666:     }
667:     if (!['security', 'logic', 'style', 'breaking-change'].includes(category)) {
668:       return [];
669:     }
670:     if (passType !== 'single') {
671:       return [];
672:     }
673: 
674:     const locationsRaw = Array.isArray(record.locations) ? record.locations : [];
675:     const locations = locationsRaw.flatMap((locationItem) => {
676:       const location =
677:         locationItem && typeof locationItem === 'object' && !Array.isArray(locationItem)
678:           ? (locationItem as Record<string, unknown>)
679:           : null;
680:       if (!location) {
681:         return [];
682:       }
683: 
684:       const filePath = typeof location.filePath === 'string' ? location.filePath.trim() : '';
685:       if (!filePath) {
686:         return [];
687:       }
688:       const startLine = location.startLine;
689:       const endLine = location.endLine;
690:       const nullRange = startLine === null && endLine === null;
691:       const numericRange =
692:         typeof startLine === 'number' &&
693:         Number.isInteger(startLine) &&
694:         startLine > 0 &&
695:         typeof endLine === 'number' &&
696:         Number.isInteger(endLine) &&
697:         endLine >= startLine;
698:       if (!nullRange && !numericRange) {
699:         return [];
700:       }
701: 
702:       return [
703:         {
704:           filePath,
705:           startLine: nullRange ? null : (startLine as number),
706:           endLine: nullRange ? null : (endLine as number),
707:         },
708:       ];
709:     });
710: 
711:     if (locations.length === 0) {
712:       return [];
713:     }
714: 
715:     return [
716:       {
717:         severity: severity as ReviewFinding['severity'],
718:         category: category as ReviewFinding['category'],
719:         passType: passType as ReviewFinding['passType'],
720:         locations,
721:         description,
722:         suggestedFix,
723:       },
724:     ];
725:   });
726: }

728: function toReviewRunResponse(record: ReviewRunRecord): ReviewRunResponse {
729:   const provenance = parseJsonOrFallback(record.provenance_json, {});
730:   const report = parseJsonOrFallback(record.report_json, null) as ReviewReport | null;
731:   const reportHasProvenance = Boolean(report?.provenance);
732: 
733:   const response: ReviewRunResponse = {
734:     id: record.id,
735:     workspaceId: record.workspace_id,
736:     deploymentId: record.deployment_id,
737:     target: {
738:       type: record.target_type,
739:       workspaceId: record.workspace_id,
740:       deploymentId: record.deployment_id,
741:     },
742:     mode: record.mode,
743:     status: record.status,
744:     idempotencyKey: record.idempotency_key,
745:     attemptCount: record.attempt_count,
746:     startedAt: record.started_at,
747:     finishedAt: record.finished_at,
748:     createdAt: record.created_at,
749:     updatedAt: record.updated_at,
750:     findings: toReviewFindingRecord(report?.findings),
751:     evidence: Array.isArray(report?.evidence) ? report.evidence : [],
752:     provenance: {
753:       sessionIds:
754:         report?.provenance && Array.isArray(report.provenance.sessionIds) ? report.provenance.sessionIds : [],
755:       promptSummary:
756:         report?.provenance && typeof report.provenance.promptSummary === 'string'
757:           ? report.provenance.promptSummary
758:           : !reportHasProvenance && typeof (provenance as Record<string, unknown>).promptSummary === 'string'
759:             ? ((provenance as Record<string, unknown>).promptSummary as string)
760:             : null,
761:       transcriptUrl:
762:         report?.provenance && typeof report.provenance.transcriptUrl === 'string'
763:           ? report.provenance.transcriptUrl
764:           : null,
765:       reviewContextRef:
766:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.reviewContextRef && typeof report.provenance.reviewContextRef === 'object'
767:           ? (report.provenance.reviewContextRef as ReviewContextRef)
768:           : null,
769:       reviewContextStats:
770:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.reviewContextStats && typeof report.provenance.reviewContextStats === 'object'
771:           ? report.provenance.reviewContextStats
772:           : undefined,
773:       coChange:
774:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.coChange && typeof report.provenance.coChange === 'object'
775:           ? report.provenance.coChange
776:           : undefined,
777:       contextResolution:
778:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.contextResolution && typeof report.provenance.contextResolution === 'object'
779:           ? report.provenance.contextResolution
780:           : undefined,
781:       outputSchemaVersion: report?.provenance?.outputSchemaVersion,
782:       passArchitecture: report?.provenance?.passArchitecture,
783:       validation:
784:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.validation && typeof report.provenance.validation === 'object'
785:           ? report.provenance.validation
786:           : undefined,
787:       furtherPassesLowYield:
788:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield && typeof report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield === 'object'
789:           ? report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield
790:           : undefined,
791:       advisories:
792:         report?.provenance && Array.isArray(report.provenance.advisories)
793:           ? report.provenance.advisories.filter((item): item is string => typeof item === 'string')
794:           : undefined,
795:     },
796:     markdownSummary: record.markdown_summary,
797:   };
798: 
799:   if (report?.summary) {
800:     response.summary = report.summary;
801:   }
802:   if (typeof report?.summaryText === 'string') {
803:     response.summaryText = report.summaryText;
804:   }
805:   if (typeof report?.furtherPassesLowYield === 'boolean') {
806:     response.furtherPassesLowYield = report.furtherPassesLowYield;
807:   }
808:   if (report?.intent) {
809:     response.intent = report.intent;
810:   }
811:   if (record.error_code && record.error_message) {
812:     response.error = {
813:       code: record.error_code,
814:       message: record.error_message,
815:     };
816:   }
817: 
818:   return response;
819: }

821: /**

823:  */
824: export async function createCheckpointJob(
825:   db: D1Database,
826:   input: CreateCheckpointJobInput
827: ): Promise<JobResponse> {
828:   const result = await db
829:     .prepare(
830:       `INSERT INTO jobs (
831:          id,
832:          prompt,
833:          model,
834:          status,
835:          phase,
836:          source_type,
837:          checkpoint_id,
838:          commit_sha,
839:          source_ref,
840:          source_project_root,
841:          build_run_tests_if_present,

847:        VALUES (?, ?, 'checkpoint', 'queued', 'queued', 'checkpoint', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
848:        RETURNING *`
849:     )
850:     .bind(
851:       input.id,
852:       input.prompt,
853:       input.checkpointId,
854:       input.commitSha,

863:     .first<JobRecord>();

907: /**
908:  * Atomically claim a queued checkpoint job for execution.
909:  * Returns true when claim succeeds, false when another worker already claimed.
910:  */
911: export async function claimQueuedCheckpointJob(db: D1Database, id: string): Promise<boolean> {
912:   const result = await db
913:     .prepare(
914:       `UPDATE jobs
915:        SET status = 'running',
916:            phase = 'building',
917:            started_at = ?,
918:            error_message = NULL,
919:            error_code = NULL
920:        WHERE id = ? AND status = 'queued'`
921:     )
922:     .bind(new Date().toISOString(), id)
923:     .run();
924: 
925:   const changes = Number((result as { meta?: { changes?: number } }).meta?.changes ?? 0);
926:   return changes > 0;
927: }

929: /**
930:  * Append a sequenced job event and return its sequence number.
931:  */
932: export async function appendJobEvent(
933:   db: D1Database,
934:   input: {
935:     jobId: string;
936:     attemptNo?: number;
937:     eventType: string;
938:     phase: JobPhase;
939:     payload: unknown;
940:   }
941: ): Promise<number> {
942:   const sequenceResult = await db
943:     .prepare('UPDATE jobs SET last_event_seq = last_event_seq + 1 WHERE id = ? RETURNING last_event_seq')
944:     .bind(input.jobId)
945:     .first<{ last_event_seq: number }>();
946: 
947:   if (!sequenceResult) {
948:     throw new Error(`Failed to allocate event sequence for job ${input.jobId}`);
949:   }
950: 
951:   const seq = Number(sequenceResult.last_event_seq);
952:   await db
953:     .prepare(
954:       `INSERT INTO job_events (job_id, attempt_no, seq, event_type, phase, payload_json)
955:        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
956:     )
957:     .bind(
958:       input.jobId,
959:       input.attemptNo ?? 0,
960:       seq,
961:       input.eventType,
962:       input.phase,
963:       JSON.stringify(input.payload)
964:     )
965:     .run();
966: 
967:   return seq;
968: }

970: /**
971:  * List persisted job events from a given sequence.
972:  */
973: export async function listJobEvents(
974:   db: D1Database,
975:   jobId: string,
976:   fromExclusive = 0,
977:   limit = 500
978: ): Promise<JobEventItem[]> {
979:   const result = await db
980:     .prepare(
981:       `SELECT id, job_id, attempt_no, seq, event_type, phase, payload_json, created_at
982:        FROM job_events
983:        WHERE job_id = ? AND seq > ?
984:        ORDER BY seq ASC
985:        LIMIT ?`
986:     )
987:     .bind(jobId, fromExclusive, limit)
988:     .all<JobEventRecord>();
989: 
990:   return result.results.map((row) => {
991:     let payload: unknown;
992:     try {
993:       payload = JSON.parse(row.payload_json);
994:     } catch {
995:       payload = { raw: row.payload_json };
996:     }
997: 
998:     return {
999:       seq: row.seq,
1000:       eventType: row.event_type,
1001:       phase: row.phase,
1002:       payload,
1003:       createdAt: row.created_at,
1004:     };
1005:   });
1006: }

1008: /**
1009:  * Update job status
1010:  */
1011: export async function updateJobStatus(
1012:   db: D1Database,
1013:   id: string,
1014:   status: JobStatus,
1015:   additionalFields?: {
1016:     phase?: JobPhase;
1017:     started_at?: string | null;
1018:     completed_at?: string | null;
1019:     cancel_requested_at?: string | null;
1020:     cancelled_at?: string | null;
1021:     preview_url?: string;
1022:     deployed_url?: string;
1023:     code_url?: string;
1024:     code_zip_url?: string;
1025:     error_message?: string | null;
1026:     error_code?: string | null;
1027:     retry_count?: number;
1028:     file_count?: number;
1029:     // Metrics fields
1030:     prompt_tokens?: number;
1031:     completion_tokens?: number;
1032:     total_tokens?: number;
1033:     cost?: number;
1034:     llm_latency_ms?: number;
1035:     install_duration_ms?: number;
1036:     build_duration_ms?: number;
1037:     deploy_duration_ms?: number;
1038:     total_duration_ms?: number;
1039:     lines_of_code?: number;
1040:   }
1041: ): Promise<void> {
1042:   const updates: string[] = ['status = ?'];
1043:   const values: (string | number | null)[] = [status];
1044: 
1045:   if (additionalFields) {
1046:     if (additionalFields.phase !== undefined) {
1047:       updates.push('phase = ?');
1048:       values.push(additionalFields.phase);
1049:     }
1050:     if (additionalFields.started_at !== undefined) {
1051:       updates.push('started_at = ?');
1052:       values.push(additionalFields.started_at);
1053:     }
1054:     if (additionalFields.completed_at !== undefined) {
1055:       updates.push('completed_at = ?');
1056:       values.push(additionalFields.completed_at);
1057:     }
1058:     if (additionalFields.cancel_requested_at !== undefined) {
1059:       updates.push('cancel_requested_at = ?');
1060:       values.push(additionalFields.cancel_requested_at);
1061:     }
1062:     if (additionalFields.cancelled_at !== undefined) {
1063:       updates.push('cancelled_at = ?');
1064:       values.push(additionalFields.cancelled_at);
1065:     }
1066:     if (additionalFields.preview_url !== undefined) {
1067:       updates.push('preview_url = ?');
1068:       values.push(additionalFields.preview_url);
1069:     }
1070:     if (additionalFields.deployed_url !== undefined) {
1071:       updates.push('deployed_url = ?');
1072:       values.push(additionalFields.deployed_url);
1073:     }
1074:     if (additionalFields.code_url !== undefined) {
1075:       updates.push('code_url = ?');
1076:       values.push(additionalFields.code_url);
1077:     }
1078:     if (additionalFields.code_zip_url !== undefined) {
1079:       updates.push('code_zip_url = ?');
1080:       values.push(additionalFields.code_zip_url);
1081:     }
1082:     if (additionalFields.error_message !== undefined) {
1083:       updates.push('error_message = ?');
1084:       values.push(additionalFields.error_message);
1085:     }
1086:     if (additionalFields.error_code !== undefined) {
1087:       updates.push('error_code = ?');
1088:       values.push(additionalFields.error_code);
1089:     }
1090:     if (additionalFields.retry_count !== undefined) {
1091:       updates.push('retry_count = ?');
1092:       values.push(additionalFields.retry_count);
1093:     }
1094:     if (additionalFields.file_count !== undefined) {
1095:       updates.push('file_count = ?');
1096:       values.push(additionalFields.file_count);
1097:     }
1098:     // Metrics fields
1099:     if (additionalFields.prompt_tokens !== undefined) {
1100:       updates.push('prompt_tokens = ?');
1101:       values.push(additionalFields.prompt_tokens);
1102:     }
1103:     if (additionalFields.completion_tokens !== undefined) {
1104:       updates.push('completion_tokens = ?');
1105:       values.push(additionalFields.completion_tokens);
1106:     }
1107:     if (additionalFields.total_tokens !== undefined) {
1108:       updates.push('total_tokens = ?');
1109:       values.push(additionalFields.total_tokens);
1110:     }
1111:     if (additionalFields.cost !== undefined) {
1112:       updates.push('cost = ?');
1113:       values.push(additionalFields.cost);
1114:     }
1115:     if (additionalFields.llm_latency_ms !== undefined) {
1116:       updates.push('llm_latency_ms = ?');
1117:       values.push(additionalFields.llm_latency_ms);
1118:     }
1119:     if (additionalFields.install_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1120:       updates.push('install_duration_ms = ?');
1121:       values.push(additionalFields.install_duration_ms);
1122:     }
1123:     if (additionalFields.build_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1124:       updates.push('build_duration_ms = ?');
1125:       values.push(additionalFields.build_duration_ms);
1126:     }
1127:     if (additionalFields.deploy_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1128:       updates.push('deploy_duration_ms = ?');
1129:       values.push(additionalFields.deploy_duration_ms);
1130:     }
1131:     if (additionalFields.total_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1132:       updates.push('total_duration_ms = ?');
1133:       values.push(additionalFields.total_duration_ms);
1134:     }
1135:     if (additionalFields.lines_of_code !== undefined) {
1136:       updates.push('lines_of_code = ?');
1137:       values.push(additionalFields.lines_of_code);
1138:     }
1139:   }
1140: 
1141:   values.push(id);
1142: 
1143:   await db
1144:     .prepare(`UPDATE jobs SET ${updates.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`)
1145:     .bind(...values)
1146:     .run();
1147: }

1222: /**
1223:  * Update workspace status and selected metadata.
1224:  */
1225: export async function updateWorkspaceStatus(
1226:   db: D1Database,
1227:   id: string,
1228:   status: WorkspaceStatus,
1229:   additionalFields?: {
1230:     baseline_ready?: number;
1231:     deleted_at?: string | null;
1232:     error_code?: string | null;
1233:     error_message?: string | null;
1234:   },
1235:   options?: {
1236:     ifNotDeleted?: boolean;
1237:   }
1238: ): Promise<boolean> {

1400:        FROM workspace_operation_idempotency

1406: 
1407:   if (existingIdempotency && existingIdempotency.expires_at > now) {

1430:   const operationId = input.id;

1434:       `INSERT INTO workspace_operations (

1448:     )

1457:       input.idempotencyKey

1461:   if (!operationRecord) {

1463:   }
1464: 
1465:   try {
1466:     await db
1467:       .prepare(
1468:         `INSERT INTO workspace_operation_idempotency (
1469:            id,
1470:            workspace_id,
1471:            operation_type,
1472:            idempotency_key,
1473:            operation_id,
1474:            request_payload_sha256,
1475:            expires_at
1476:          )
1477:          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
1478:       )
1479:       .bind(
1480:         generatePrefixedId('wopk'),
1481:         input.workspaceId,
1482:         input.type,
1483:         input.idempotencyKey,
1484:         operationId,
1485:         input.requestPayloadSha256,

1487:       )

1495:     const concurrentIdempotency = await db
1496:       .prepare(
1497:         `SELECT operation_id, request_payload_sha256, expires_at
1498:          FROM workspace_operation_idempotency

1585:   if (options?.finishedAt !== undefined) {
1586:     updates.push('finished_at = ?');
1587:     values.push(options.finishedAt);
1588:   }
1589:   if (options?.result !== undefined) {
1590:     updates.push('result_json = ?');
1591:     values.push(JSON.stringify(options.result));
1592:   }
1593:   if (options?.warnings !== undefined) {
1594:     updates.push('warnings_json = ?');
1595:     values.push(JSON.stringify(options.warnings));
1596:   }
1597:   if (options?.errorCode !== undefined) {
1598:     updates.push('error_code = ?');
1599:     values.push(options.errorCode);
1600:   }
1601:   if (options?.errorClass !== undefined) {
1602:     updates.push('error_class = ?');
1603:     values.push(options.errorClass);
1604:   }
1605:   if (options?.errorMessage !== undefined) {
1606:     updates.push('error_message = ?');
1607:     values.push(options.errorMessage);
1608:   }
1609:   if (options?.errorDetails !== undefined) {
1610:     updates.push('error_details_json = ?');
1611:     values.push(JSON.stringify(options.errorDetails));
1612:   }
1613:   if (status === 'running') {
1614:     updates.push('started_at = COALESCE(started_at, ?)');
1615:     values.push(new Date().toISOString());
1616:   }
1617:   if (status === 'succeeded' || status === 'failed') {

134: class ReviewPolicyError extends Error {
135:   constructor(message: string) {
136:     super(message);
137:     this.name = 'ReviewPolicyError';
138:   }
139: }
140: 
141: function shellQuote(value: string): string {
142:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, `"'"'`)}'`;
143: }
144: 
145: function parseIntegerString(value: string | undefined, fallback: number, min: number, max: number): number {
146:   if (value === undefined) {
147:     return fallback;
148:   }
149:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(value.trim(), 10);
150:   if (Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
151:     return fallback;
152:   }
153:   return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, parsed));
154: }
155: 
156: function readOptionalString(value: unknown): string | null {
157:   return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() ? value.trim() : null;
158: }
159: 
160: function asRecord(value: unknown): Record<string, unknown> {
161:   return value && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value) ? (value as Record<string, unknown>) : {};
162: }
163: 
164: function asStringArray(value: unknown): string[] {
165:   return Array.isArray(value) ? value.filter((item): item is string => typeof item === 'string') : [];
166: }
167: 
168: function boundedJson(value: unknown, maxBytes: number): string {
169:   const serialized = JSON.stringify(value);
170:   const clamped = clampText(serialized, maxBytes);
171:   return clamped.truncated ? `${clamped.text} [TRUNCATED]` : clamped.text;
172: }
173: 
174: function stripCodeFences(value: string): string {
175:   const trimmed = value.trim();
176:   const fenced = trimmed.match(/^```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```$/i);
177:   return fenced ? fenced[1].trim() : trimmed;
178: }
179: 
180: function extractJsonObject(value: string): string {
181:   const stripped = stripCodeFences(value);
182:   const start = stripped.indexOf('{');
183:   const end = stripped.lastIndexOf('}');
184:   if (start >= 0 && end > start) {
185:     return stripped.slice(start, end + 1);
186:   }
187:   return stripped;
188: }
189: 
190: function normalizeIntent(value: unknown): ReviewAgentIntent | null {
191:   const record = asRecord(value);
192:   if (Object.keys(record).length === 0) {
193:     return null;
194:   }
195: 
196:   return {
197:     goal: typeof record.goal === 'string' && record.goal.trim() ? record.goal.trim() : null,
198:     constraints: asStringArray(record.constraints).map((item) => item.trim()).filter(Boolean),
199:     decisions: asStringArray(record.decisions).map((item) => item.trim()).filter(Boolean),
200:   };
201: }

203: class ReviewAgentOutputError extends Error {
204:   code: string;
205:   details: Record<string, unknown> | null;
206: 
207:   constructor(message: string) {
208:     super(message);
209:     this.name = 'ReviewAgentOutputError';
210:     this.code = 'review_analysis_invalid_output';
211:     this.details = null;
212:   }
213: 
214:   withCode(code: string, details?: Record<string, unknown>): ReviewAgentOutputError {
215:     this.code = code;
216:     this.details = details ?? null;
217:     return this;
218:   }
219: }
220: 
221: function parseJsonOutput(summary: string): unknown {
222:   try {
223:     return JSON.parse(extractJsonObject(summary)) as unknown;
224:   } catch {
225:     const summaryText = stripCodeFences(summary).trim();
226:     throw new ReviewAgentOutputError(
227:       summaryText
228:         ? `Review agent returned malformed final output: ${summaryText.slice(0, 240)}`
229:         : 'Review agent returned malformed final output'
230:     ).withCode('review_analysis_invalid_output');
231:   }
232: }
233: 
234: function validateOutputOrThrow(payload: unknown): { output: ReviewAnalysisOutputV2; dedupedExactCount: number } {
235:   const validated = validateAndNormalizeReviewAnalysisOutputV2(payload);
236:   if (!validated.ok) {
237:     throw new ReviewAgentOutputError('Review agent output does not match required schema').withCode(
238:       'review_analysis_invalid_output',
239:       {
240:         errors: validated.errors,
241:       }
242:     );
243:   }
244:   return {
245:     output: validated.value,
246:     dedupedExactCount: validated.dedupedExactCount,
247:   };
248: }
249: 
250: function extractValidationErrors(error: ReviewAgentOutputError | null): Array<{ path: string; message: string }> {
251:   if (!error?.details || !Array.isArray(error.details.errors)) {
252:     return [];
253:   }
254:   return error.details.errors
255:     .map((item) => {
256:       const record = asRecord(item);
257:       const path = typeof record.path === 'string' ? record.path : '';
258:       const message = typeof record.message === 'string' ? record.message : '';
259:       return path && message ? { path, message } : null;
260:     })
261:     .filter((item): item is { path: string; message: string } => Boolean(item))
262:     .slice(0, 20);
263: }

265: function buildFallbackAnalysisOutput(reason: string): ReviewAnalysisOutputV2 {
266:   return {
267:     findings: [],
268:     summary: `Structured model output was invalid after repair (${reason}); emitted fallback empty findings output.`,
269:     furtherPassesLowYield: true,
270:   };
271: }

273: function isGenericProviderCompletionSummary(summary: string): boolean {
274:   return /completed by .*agent endpoint/i.test(summary.trim());

277: function sanitizeErrorMessage(input: string): string {
278:   return redactReviewText(input) ?? '';

281: function isWorkerToWorkerFetchRestriction(status: number, responseBody: string): boolean {
282:   return status === 404 && /error\s*code\s*:\s*1042/i.test(responseBody);
283: }

285: function isTimeoutLikeError(error: unknown): boolean {
286:   if (!(error instanceof Error)) {
287:     return false;
288:   }
289:   if (error.name === 'AbortError') {
290:     return true;
291:   }
292:   const message = error.message.toLowerCase();
293:   return message.includes('timeout') || message.includes('timed out') || message.includes('aborted');
294: }

296: function redactReviewText(value: string | null): string | null {
297:   if (!value) {
298:     return null;
299:   }
300: 
301:   const redacted = value
302:     .replace(/(authorization:\s*bearer\s+)[a-z0-9._-]+/gi, '$1[REDACTED]')
303:     .replace(GITHUB_TOKEN_PATTERN, '[REDACTED_TOKEN]')
304:     .replace(/((?:"|')?api[_-]?key(?:"|')?\s*[:=]\s*)("[^"]*"|'[^']*'|[^\s,}]+)/gi, '$1[REDACTED]')
305:     .replace(/((?:"|')?token(?:"|')?\s*[:=]\s*)("[^"]*"|'[^']*'|[^\s,}]+)/gi, '$1[REDACTED]');
306:   return redacted.length > 600 ? `${redacted.slice(0, 597)}...` : redacted;
307: }
308: 
309: function toBase64(buffer: ArrayBuffer): string {
310:   const bytes = new Uint8Array(buffer);
311:   let binary = '';
312:   for (const byte of bytes) {
313:     binary += String.fromCharCode(byte);
314:   }
315:   return btoa(binary);
316: }
317: 
318: async function writeBundleBase64InChunks(sandbox: SandboxClient, bundleBytes: ArrayBuffer): Promise<void> {
319:   const bytes = new Uint8Array(bundleBytes);
320:   await runSandboxCommand(sandbox, `rm -f ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PATH)} ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PART_PREFIX)}*`);
321: 
322:   let partIndex = 0;
323:   for (let offset = 0; offset < bytes.byteLength; offset += BUNDLE_BASE64_CHUNK_BYTES) {
324:     const chunk = bytes.subarray(offset, Math.min(offset + BUNDLE_BASE64_CHUNK_BYTES, bytes.byteLength));
325:     const chunkBase64 = toBase64(chunk.buffer.slice(chunk.byteOffset, chunk.byteOffset + chunk.byteLength));
326:     const partPath = `${BUNDLE_BASE64_PART_PREFIX}.${String(partIndex).padStart(4, '0')}`;
327:     await sandbox.writeFile(partPath, chunkBase64);
328:     partIndex += 1;
329:   }
330: 
331:   await runSandboxCommand(sandbox, `cat ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PART_PREFIX)}.* > ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PATH)}`);
332: }
333: 
334: async function hydrateReviewSandbox(sandbox: SandboxClient, sourceBytes: ArrayBuffer): Promise<void> {
335:   await runSandboxCommand(sandbox, `rm -rf ${shellQuote(WORKSPACE_ROOT)} && mkdir -p ${shellQuote(WORKSPACE_ROOT)}`);
336:   await writeBundleBase64InChunks(sandbox, sourceBytes);
337:   await runSandboxCommand(
338:     sandbox,
339:     `base64 -d ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PATH)} > ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_PATH)} && tar -xzf ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_PATH)} -C ${shellQuote(WORKSPACE_ROOT)}`
340:   );
341:   await runSandboxCommand(
342:     sandbox,
343:     `rm -f ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PATH)} ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_PATH)} ${shellQuote(BUNDLE_BASE64_PART_PREFIX)}*`
344:   );
345: }

359: function buildReviewAgentPrompt(input: ReviewAgentPromptInput): string {
360:   const promptDiffSnapshot = input.authoritativeDiffSnapshot !== undefined
361:     ? clampAuthoritativeDiffSnapshot(input.authoritativeDiffSnapshot, 32_000)
362:     : undefined;
363:   const context = input.reviewContext;
364: 
365:   const changedFiles = context.retrieval.changedFiles.map((file) => ({
366:     path: file.path,
367:     content: file.content,
368:     byteSize: file.byteSize,
369:   }));
370:   const relatedFiles = context.retrieval.relatedFiles.map((file) => ({
371:     path: file.path,
372:     content: file.content,
373:     byteSize: file.byteSize,
374:     coChangeFrequency: file.coChangeFrequency,
375:     supportingSessionIds: file.supportingSessionIds,
376:   }));
377:   const conventionFiles = context.retrieval.conventionFiles.map((file) => ({
378:     path: file.path,
379:     content: file.content,
380:     byteSize: file.byteSize,
381:   }));
382: 
383:   return [
384:     'You are Nimbus Review, a non-mutating code review agent.',
385:     'Analyze the provided full ReviewContext and return only actionable findings supported by code context.',
386:     'Never propose edits or run mutating commands. Prefer no findings over weak findings.',
387:     'Use only these tools when needed: list_files, read_file, diff_summary.',
388:     '',
389:     'Return your final answer as raw JSON with this shape and no surrounding prose:',
390:     '{',
391:     '  "findings": [',
392:     '    {',
393:     '      "severity": "info|low|medium|high|critical",',
394:     '      "category": "security|logic|style|breaking-change",',
395:     '      "passType": "single|security|logic|style|breaking-change",',
396:     '      "locations": [{ "filePath": string, "startLine": number|null, "endLine": number|null }],',
397:     '      "description": string,',
398:     '      "suggestedFix": string',
399:     '    }',
400:     '  ],',
401:     '  "summary": string,',
402:     '  "furtherPassesLowYield": boolean',
403:     '}',
404:     '',
405:     'Critical field typing requirements:',
406:     '- summary must be a plain JSON string value (not an object, array, number, or nested structure).',
407:     '- furtherPassesLowYield must be exactly true or false (JSON boolean only; do not use strings like "true"/"false" and do not use null).',
408:     '- Do not return summary as an object (for example with riskLevel/recommendation).',
409:     '- Do not return summaryText or any alternate summary field; only return summary.',
410:     '',
411:     'Phase 2 constraints:',
412:     '- passType must be set explicitly on every finding and be "single" in this phase.',
413:     '- category must be one of security, logic, style, breaking-change.',
414:     '- locations must contain at least one location; file-only location is allowed with null line values.',
415:     '- findings must be self-contained and actionable without rereading the diff.',
416:     '',
417:     'Context weighting rules:',
418:     '- Changed files are directly modified in this commit and should get highest weight.',
419:     '- Related files are historical co-change context from Entire checkpoint session history and are not directly modified in this commit.',
420:     '- Use related files to understand coupling/impact, but do not treat them as direct modified evidence.',
421:     '',
422:     `Review Context ID: ${context.id}`,
423:     `Review ID: ${input.reviewId}`,
424:     `Workspace ID: ${input.workspaceId}`,
425:     `Deployment ID: ${input.deploymentId}`,
426:     `Checkpoint: ${context.checkpoint.checkpointId} (${context.checkpoint.branch})`,
427:     `Checkpoint Session ID: ${context.checkpoint.session.sessionId}`,
428:     `Goal: ${input.goal}`,
429:     `Constraints: ${JSON.stringify(input.constraints)}`,
430:     `Decisions: ${JSON.stringify(input.decisions)}`,
431:     input.intentSessionContext.length > 0
432:       ? `Intent session context excerpts: ${JSON.stringify(input.intentSessionContext)}`
433:       : 'Intent session context excerpts: []',
434:     `Deployment summary: ${JSON.stringify(input.deploymentSummary)}`,
435:     `Evidence catalog: ${JSON.stringify(input.evidenceCatalog)}`,
436:     '',
437:     `Diff hunks (direct changes): ${boundedJson(context.retrieval.diffHunks, PROMPT_DIFF_HUNKS_MAX_BYTES)}`,
438:     `Changed files (directly modified): ${boundedJson(changedFiles, PROMPT_CHANGED_FILES_MAX_BYTES)}`,
439:     `Related files (historical co-change context only): ${boundedJson(relatedFiles, PROMPT_RELATED_FILES_MAX_BYTES)}`,
440:     `Convention/config files: ${boundedJson(conventionFiles, PROMPT_CONVENTION_FILES_MAX_BYTES)}`,
441:     `Co-change retrieval stats: ${JSON.stringify(context.retrieval.coChange)}`,
442:     `Review context stats: ${JSON.stringify(context.stats)}`,
443:     promptDiffSnapshot !== undefined
444:       ? `Authoritative deployed diff snapshot: ${JSON.stringify(promptDiffSnapshot)}`
445:       : 'Authoritative deployed diff snapshot: unavailable',
446:     `Initial root listing: ${JSON.stringify(input.rootListing)}`,
447:     `Initial diff snapshot: ${JSON.stringify(input.diffSnapshot)}`,
448:     '',
449:     'If you cannot justify a concrete issue, return an empty findings array with a concise summary.',
450:   ].join('\n');
451: }

453: function clampText(value: string, maxBytes: number): { text: string; truncated: boolean } {
454:   const encoder = new TextEncoder();
455:   const bytes = encoder.encode(value);
456:   if (bytes.byteLength <= maxBytes) {
457:     return { text: value, truncated: false };
458:   }
459:   const sliced = bytes.slice(0, Math.max(0, maxBytes - 3));
460:   return { text: new TextDecoder().decode(sliced) + '...', truncated: true };
461: }
462: 
463: function clampAuthoritativeDiffSnapshot(value: unknown, maxBytes: number): unknown {
464:   const record = asRecord(value);
465:   if (Object.keys(record).length === 0) {
466:     return value;
467:   }
468: 
469:   const patch = typeof record.patch === 'string' ? record.patch : null;
470:   if (!patch) {
471:     return value;
472:   }
473: 
474:   const clamped = clampText(patch, maxBytes);
475:   return {
476:     ...record,
477:     patch: clamped.text,
478:     truncated: clamped.truncated || Boolean(record.truncated),
479:   };
480: }
481: 
482: function sanitizeToolValue(value: unknown): unknown {
483:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
484:     return redactReviewText(value) ?? '';
485:   }
486:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
487:     return value.map((item) => sanitizeToolValue(item));
488:   }
489:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
490:     return Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>).reduce<Record<string, unknown>>((result, [key, nested]) => {
491:       result[key] = sanitizeToolValue(nested);
492:       return result;
493:     }, {});
494:   }
495:   return value;
496: }
497: 
498: function sanitizeToolContext(context: ReviewToolContext): ReviewToolContext {
499:   return {
500:     request: sanitizeToolValue(context.request) as Record<string, unknown>,
501:     result: sanitizeToolValue(context.result),
502:   };
503: }

509: function buildListFilesCommand(absolutePath: string, rootPath: string): string {
510:   return `python3 - ${shellQuote(absolutePath)} ${shellQuote(rootPath)} <<'PY'
511: import json
512: import os
513: import sys
514: 
515: path = sys.argv[1]
516: root = sys.argv[2]
517: root_real = os.path.realpath(root)
518: target = path if os.path.isabs(path) else os.path.join(root, path)
519: target_real = os.path.realpath(target)
520: if os.path.commonpath([root_real, target_real]) != root_real:
521:     print(json.dumps({'error':'path_escape'}))
522:     raise SystemExit(0)
523: if not os.path.exists(target_real):
524:     print(json.dumps({'error':'not_found'}))
525:     raise SystemExit(0)
526: if not os.path.isdir(target_real):
527:     print(json.dumps({'error':'not_directory'}))
528:     raise SystemExit(0)
529: entries = []
530: for name in sorted(os.listdir(target_real)):
531:     full = os.path.join(target_real, name)
532:     entries.append({'name': name, 'type': 'directory' if os.path.isdir(full) else 'file'})
533: print(json.dumps({'entries': entries[:200]}))
534: PY`;
535: }
536: 
537: function buildReadFileCommand(absolutePath: string, maxBytes: number, rootPath: string): string {
538:   return `python3 - ${shellQuote(absolutePath)} ${maxBytes} ${shellQuote(rootPath)} <<'PY'
539: import json
540: import os
541: import sys
542: 
543: path = sys.argv[1]
544: max_bytes = int(sys.argv[2])
545: root = sys.argv[3]
546: root_real = os.path.realpath(root)
547: target = path if os.path.isabs(path) else os.path.join(root, path)
548: target_real = os.path.realpath(target)
549: if os.path.commonpath([root_real, target_real]) != root_real:
550:     print(json.dumps({'error':'path_escape'}))
551:     raise SystemExit(0)
552: if not os.path.exists(target_real):
553:     print(json.dumps({'error':'not_found'}))
554:     raise SystemExit(0)
555: if not os.path.isfile(target_real):
556:     print(json.dumps({'error':'not_file'}))
557:     raise SystemExit(0)
558: with open(target_real, 'rb') as f:
559:     data = f.read(max_bytes + 1)
560: truncated = len(data) > max_bytes
561: if truncated:
562:     data = data[:max_bytes]
563: text = data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
564: print(json.dumps({'content': text, 'truncated': truncated, 'bytes': len(data)}))
565: PY`;
566: }

568: function assertWorkspacePath(pathInput: string, policy: ReviewCommandPolicy): string {
569:   const trimmed = (pathInput || '.').trim();
570:   const normalized = trimmed.replace(/\\/g, '/');
571:   if (normalized.includes('\u0000')) {
572:     throw new ReviewPolicyError('Path contains null bytes');
573:   }
574:   if (normalized.startsWith('/') || normalized.startsWith('..') || normalized.includes('/../')) {
575:     throw new ReviewPolicyError('Path escapes workspace root');
576:   }
577:   const collapsed = normalized
578:     .split('/')
579:     .filter((segment) => segment.length > 0 && segment !== '.')
580:     .join('/');
581:   if (collapsed.startsWith('..')) {
582:     throw new ReviewPolicyError('Path escapes workspace root');
583:   }
584:   if (collapsed === '.git' || collapsed.startsWith('.git/')) {
585:     throw new ReviewPolicyError('Access to .git is denied by policy');
586:   }
587:   return `${policy.rootPath}/${collapsed}`;
588: }
589: 
590: async function runSandboxCommand(
591:   sandbox: SandboxClient,
592:   command: string,
593:   timeout?: number
594: ): Promise<{ stdout: string; stderr: string }> {
595:   const result = await sandbox.exec(command, { timeout });
596:   if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
597:     const output = [result.stdout, result.stderr].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
598:     throw new Error(`Sandbox command failed with exit ${result.exitCode}: ${output || 'No output'}`);
599:   }
600:   return { stdout: result.stdout, stderr: result.stderr };
601: }
602: 
603: async function executeReviewTool(
604:   sandbox: SandboxClient,
605:   action: Extract<ReviewAgentAction, { type: 'tool' }>,
606:   policy: ReviewCommandPolicy,
607:   maxFileBytes: number,
608:   authoritativeDiffSnapshot?: unknown
609: ): Promise<ReviewToolContext> {
610:   if (action.tool === 'list_files') {
611:     const absolutePath = assertWorkspacePath(action.args.path ?? '.', policy);
612:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
613:       sandbox,
614:       buildListFilesCommand(absolutePath, policy.rootPath)
615:     );
616:     return {
617:       request: { path: action.args.path ?? '.' },
618:       result: JSON.parse(output.stdout || '{}'),
619:     };
620:   }
621: 
622:   if (action.tool === 'read_file') {
623:     const absolutePath = assertWorkspacePath(action.args.path, policy);
624:     const maxBytes = typeof action.args.maxBytes === 'number' && Number.isFinite(action.args.maxBytes)
625:       ? Math.max(1, Math.min(maxFileBytes, Math.floor(action.args.maxBytes)))
626:       : maxFileBytes;
627:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
628:       sandbox,
629:       buildReadFileCommand(absolutePath, maxBytes, policy.rootPath)
630:     );
631:     return {
632:       request: { path: action.args.path, maxBytes },
633:       result: JSON.parse(output.stdout || '{}'),
634:     };
635:   }
636: 
637:   if (action.tool === 'run_command') {
638:     return {
639:       request: { command: action.args.command, timeoutMs: action.args.timeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS },
640:       result: {
641:         error: 'run_command is disabled in review mode; use list_files, read_file, or diff_summary only',
642:         disabled: true,
643:       },
644:     };
645:   }
646: 
647:   if (action.tool === 'write_file') {
648:     return {
649:       request: { path: action.args.path },
650:       result: {
651:         ok: false,
652:         error: 'write_file is disabled in review mode; use read-only tools and return findings only',
653:         path: action.args.path,
654:       },
655:     };
656:   }
657: 
658:   if (authoritativeDiffSnapshot !== undefined) {
659:     const maxBytes = typeof action.args.maxBytes === 'number' && Number.isFinite(action.args.maxBytes)
660:       ? Math.max(1_024, Math.min(policy.maxOutputBytes, Math.floor(action.args.maxBytes)))
661:       : Math.min(policy.maxOutputBytes, 64_000);
662:     return {
663:       request: { maxBytes },
664:       result: clampAuthoritativeDiffSnapshot(authoritativeDiffSnapshot, maxBytes),
665:     };
666:   }
667: 
668:   return {
669:     request: { maxBytes: typeof action.args.maxBytes === 'number' ? action.args.maxBytes : undefined },
670:     result: {
671:       error: 'authoritative diff snapshot unavailable',
672:       changedFiles: [],
673:       patch: '',
674:       truncated: false,
675:     },
676:   };
677: }

679: class CloudflareAgentSdkReviewProvider implements ReviewAgentProvider {
680:   constructor(
681:     private readonly endpoint: string,
682:     private readonly authToken: string | null,
683:     private readonly serviceBinding: Fetcher | null
684:   ) {}
685: 
686:   async next(input: {
687:     prompt: string;
688:     model: string;
689:     maxSteps: number;
690:     step: number;
691:     history: ReviewAgentHistoryEntry[];
692:   }): Promise<ReviewAgentAction> {
693:     let response: Response;
694:     try {
695:       const requestFetch = this.serviceBinding
696:         ? this.serviceBinding.fetch.bind(this.serviceBinding)
697:         : fetch;
698:       const signal =
699:         typeof AbortSignal.timeout === 'function'
700:           ? AbortSignal.timeout(REVIEW_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS)
701:           : (() => {
702:               const controller = new AbortController();
703:               setTimeout(() => controller.abort('review_provider_timeout'), REVIEW_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS);
704:               return controller.signal;
705:             })();
706:       response = await requestFetch(this.endpoint, {
707:         method: 'POST',
708:         headers: {
709:           'Content-Type': 'application/json',
710:           ...(this.authToken ? { Authorization: `Bearer ${this.authToken}` } : {}),
711:         },
712:         body: JSON.stringify({
713:           mode: 'workspace_task',
714:           prompt: input.prompt,
715:           model: input.model,
716:           maxSteps: input.maxSteps,
717:           step: input.step,
718:           history: input.history,
719:         }),
720:         signal,
721:       });
722:     } catch (error) {
723:       if (isTimeoutLikeError(error)) {
724:         throw new Error(
725:           `Review analysis provider request timed out after ${Math.floor(REVIEW_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS / 1000)} seconds`
726:         );
727:       }
728:       throw error;
729:     }
730: 
731:     if (!response.ok) {
732:       const providerErrorBody = redactReviewText(await response.text()) ?? '';
733:       if (isWorkerToWorkerFetchRestriction(response.status, providerErrorBody)) {
734:         throw new Error(
735:           `Review analysis provider request blocked by Cloudflare Worker-to-Worker fetch restriction (error code 1042). Enable the 'global_fetch_strictly_public' compatibility flag on this worker or switch to a service binding.${providerErrorBody ? ` Provider response: ${providerErrorBody}` : ''}`
736:         );
737:       }
738:       if (response.status >= 500 || response.status === 429) {
739:         throw new Error(
740:           `Review analysis provider temporarily unavailable (status ${response.status})${providerErrorBody ? `: ${providerErrorBody}` : ''}`
741:         );
742:       }
743:       throw new Error(
744:         `Review analysis provider request failed with status ${response.status}${providerErrorBody ? `: ${providerErrorBody}` : ''}`
745:       );
746:     }
747: 
748:     const parsed = (await response.json()) as unknown;
749:     const action = asRecord(parsed).action;
750:     return validateReviewAgentAction(action);
751:   }
752: }

754: function validateReviewAgentAction(action: unknown): ReviewAgentAction {
755:   const record = asRecord(action);
756:   if (record.type === 'final') {
757:     const summary = typeof record.summary === 'string' ? record.summary.trim() : '';
758:     if (!summary) {
759:       throw new ReviewPolicyError('Final action requires a non-empty summary');
760:     }
761:     return { type: 'final', summary };
762:   }
763:   if (record.type !== 'tool') {
764:     throw new ReviewPolicyError('Action type must be tool or final');
765:   }
766: 
767:   const tool = typeof record.tool === 'string' ? record.tool : '';
768:   const args = asRecord(record.args);
769:   switch (tool) {
770:     case 'list_files':
771:       if (args.path !== undefined && typeof args.path !== 'string') {
772:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('list_files.path must be a string when provided');
773:       }
774:       return { type: 'tool', tool, args };
775:     case 'read_file':
776:       if (typeof args.path !== 'string' || !args.path.trim()) {
777:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('read_file.path is required');
778:       }
779:       if (args.maxBytes !== undefined && (typeof args.maxBytes !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.maxBytes))) {
780:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('read_file.maxBytes must be a number when provided');
781:       }
782:       return { type: 'tool', tool, args: { path: args.path, maxBytes: args.maxBytes as number | undefined } };
783:     case 'write_file':
784:       if (typeof args.path !== 'string' || !args.path.trim()) {
785:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('write_file.path is required');
786:       }
787:       return { type: 'tool', tool, args: { path: args.path, content: typeof args.content === 'string' ? args.content : undefined } };
788:     case 'run_command':
789:       if (typeof args.command !== 'string' || !args.command.trim()) {
790:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('run_command.command is required');
791:       }
792:       if (args.timeoutMs !== undefined && (typeof args.timeoutMs !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.timeoutMs))) {
793:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('run_command.timeoutMs must be a number when provided');
794:       }
795:       return { type: 'tool', tool, args: { command: args.command, timeoutMs: args.timeoutMs as number | undefined } };
796:     case 'diff_summary':
797:       if (args.maxBytes !== undefined && (typeof args.maxBytes !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.maxBytes))) {
798:         throw new ReviewPolicyError('diff_summary.maxBytes must be a number when provided');
799:       }
800:       return { type: 'tool', tool, args: { maxBytes: args.maxBytes as number | undefined } };
801:     default:
802:       throw new ReviewPolicyError(`Tool '${tool}' is not supported in review mode`);
803:   }
804: }

846: export async function readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(
847:   env: Env,
848:   input: {
849:     sourceBundleKey: string;
850:     sandboxId: string;
851:     paths: string[];
852:     maxFileBytes?: number;
853:   }
854: ): Promise<ReviewSourceFileReadResult[]> {
855:   const maxFileBytes = parseIntegerString(env.REVIEW_AGENT_MAX_FILE_BYTES, DEFAULT_REVIEW_MAX_FILE_BYTES, 1_024, 200_000);
856:   const effectiveMaxFileBytes = typeof input.maxFileBytes === 'number' && Number.isFinite(input.maxFileBytes)
857:     ? Math.max(1_024, Math.min(maxFileBytes, Math.floor(input.maxFileBytes)))
858:     : maxFileBytes;
859: 
860:   if (!env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS && !env.SOURCE_BUNDLES) {
861:     throw new Error('WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS or SOURCE_BUNDLES binding is required for review context assembly');
862:   }
863:   const bundle =
864:     (env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS ? await env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS.get(input.sourceBundleKey) : null) ??
865:     (env.SOURCE_BUNDLES ? await env.SOURCE_BUNDLES.get(input.sourceBundleKey) : null);
866:   if (!bundle) {
867:     throw new Error(`Review source bundle not found: ${input.sourceBundleKey}`);
868:   }
869: 
870:   const sandbox = await sandboxResolver(env, input.sandboxId);
871:   try {
872:     await hydrateReviewSandbox(sandbox, await bundle.arrayBuffer());
873: 
874:     const policy: ReviewCommandPolicy = {
875:       commandAllow: [],
876:       commandDeny: [],
877:       maxCommandTimeoutMs: MAX_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS,
878:       maxOutputBytes: DEFAULT_REVIEW_MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES,
879:       rootPath: WORKSPACE_ROOT,
880:     };

882:     const uniquePaths = Array.from(new Set(input.paths.map((path) => path.trim()).filter(Boolean)));
883:     const results: ReviewSourceFileReadResult[] = [];
884:     for (const path of uniquePaths) {
885:       const toolResult = await executeReviewTool(

888:         policy,
889:         effectiveMaxFileBytes
890:       );
891:       const resultRecord = asRecord(toolResult.result);
892:       const error = typeof resultRecord.error === 'string' ? resultRecord.error : null;
893:       const content = typeof resultRecord.content === 'string' ? resultRecord.content : null;
894:       const bytes = typeof resultRecord.bytes === 'number' && Number.isFinite(resultRecord.bytes)
895:         ? Math.max(0, Math.floor(resultRecord.bytes))
896:         : 0;
897:       const truncated = Boolean(resultRecord.truncated);

899:         path,
900:         content,
901:         bytes,
902:         truncated,
903:         error,

909:     if (typeof sandbox.destroy === 'function') {
910:       try {
911:         await sandbox.destroy();
912:       } catch {

914:       }
915:     }
916:   }

985:     const history: ReviewAgentHistoryEntry[] = [];

1092:             });
1093:             history.push({
1094:               role: 'tool',
1095:               tool: 'final_output_validator',
1096:               output: {
1097:                 ok: false,
1098:                 error: 'Output must match ReviewAnalysisOutputV2 exactly.',
1099:                 requiredShape: {
1100:                   findings: [
1101:                     {
1102:                       severity: 'info|low|medium|high|critical',
1103:                       category: 'security|logic|style|breaking-change',
1104:                       passType: 'single|security|logic|style|breaking-change',
1105:                       locations: [{ filePath: 'string', startLine: 'number|null', endLine: 'number|null' }],
1106:                       description: 'string',

<path>/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Nimbus
2: 
3: Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.
4: 
5: This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.
6: 
7: Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)
8: 
9: ## Current Focus
10: 
11: Nimbus currently supports a cloud review/deploy workflow built around immutable source snapshots:
12: 
13: - Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
14: - Create a workspace from that snapshot
15: - Run deploy validation and deployment inside Cloudflare Sandbox
16: - Persist replayable deploy/review lifecycle state in D1
17: - Generate non-mutating deployment-backed review reports
18: 
19: ## What Works Today
20: 
21: - Checkpoint/commit-backed workspace creation
22: - Queue-backed workspace task and workspace deploy processing
23: - Deploy preflight with toolchain / git baseline / secret scan checks
24: - Deployment lifecycle tracking with replayable events
25: - Non-mutating review lifecycle with persisted reports and live SSE events
26: - Minimal report viewer UI (`/reports/:reviewId`) with copy/download workflows
27: - CLI flows for:
28:   - `workspace create`
29:   - `workspace show`
30:   - `workspace deploy`
31:   - `review create`
32:   - `review events`
33:   - `review show`
34:   - `review export`
35: 
36: Entire checkpoint notes:
37: 
38: - Checkpoint IDs from commit trailers (for example `checkpoint:be1b10a00b44`) resolve and run.
39: - The worker executes install/build/test/lint in Cloudflare Sandbox for that checkpoint source.
40: 
41: ## Known Limits (Expected Right Now)
42: 
43: - Review quality still depends heavily on the external agent/provider output
44: - `workspace_deployment` is the only review target in this slice
45: - Simulated deploy provider returns a synthetic deployed URL unless real provider mode is enabled
46: - `workspace create HEAD` uses committed `HEAD`, not uncommitted local changes
47: - Report UI is single-report only (no report index/history view yet)
48: - Report UI V1 has no auth/SSO (reviewId-access)
49: 
50: ## Quick Start (Dev)
51: 
52: From repo root:
53: 
54: ```bash
55: nvm use
56: source ~/.bash_profile
57: pnpm install
58: ```
59: 
60: Set up infra (safe to re-run):
61: 
62: ```bash
63: pnpm run setup:worker
64: ```
65: 
66: Deploy worker:
67: 
68: ```bash
69: pnpm run deploy
70: ```
71: 
72: Point CLI to your worker URL:
73: 
74: ```bash
75: export NIMBUS_WORKER_URL="https://<your-worker>.workers.dev"
76: ```
77: 
78: ## Quick Start (Cloud Flow)
79: 
80: Run this from repo root to exercise the deployed worker + cloud sandbox flow:
81: 
82: ```bash
83: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev workspace create HEAD
84: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev workspace show <workspace-id>
85: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev workspace deploy <workspace-id> --no-tests --no-build
86: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev review create --workspace <workspace-id> --deployment <deployment-id>
87: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev review events <review-id>
88: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev review show <review-id>
89: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus dev review export <review-id> --format markdown --out /tmp/review.md
90: ```
91: 
92: Notes:
93: 
94: - The CLI runs locally; workspace/deploy/review execution happens in the cloud worker + sandbox.
95: - If deploy preflight fails because validation tooling is missing in the sandbox, use `--no-tests --no-build` for the manual flow.
96: - If deploy preflight reports a missing git baseline, retry with `--auto-fix` or reset/recreate the workspace.
97: 
98: ## Quick Start (Report UI V1)
99: 
100: Start worker + UI locally from repo root:
101: 
102: ```bash
103: pnpm dev
104: pnpm dev:report-ui
105: ```
106: 
107: Then open:
108: 
109: ```text
110: http://localhost:5173/reports/<review-id>
111: ```
112: 
113: Local API routing defaults:
114: 
115: - Vite proxies `/api/*` to `http://127.0.0.1:8787` by default.
116: - Override proxy target with `NIMBUS_API_PROXY_TARGET`.
117: - Or set `VITE_NIMBUS_API_BASE_URL` to call a hosted worker directly.
118: 
119: Hosted worker example:
120: 
121: ```bash
122: VITE_NIMBUS_API_BASE_URL="https://<your-worker>.workers.dev" pnpm dev:report-ui
123: ```
124: 
125: Report UI V1 includes:
126: 
127: - Summary header (recommendation, risk, findings count, status, timestamps)
128: - Findings cards with per-finding copy and fix-prompt copy
129: - Rendered markdown summary (sanitized)
130: - Raw JSON section (collapsible)
131: - Copy/download actions for full markdown and full JSON
132: 
133: Create and watch a live checkpoint job:
134: 
135: ```bash
136: pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint checkpoint:be1b10a00b44 --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
137: pnpm cli -- watch <job_id>
138: ```
139: 
140: You can also target any commit-ish:
141: 
142: ```bash
143: pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint HEAD --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
144: ```
145: 
146: ## Common Commands
147: 
148: ```bash
149: # Run worker tests
150: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus-worker test
151: 
152: # Run CLI tests
153: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus test
154: 
155: # Run worker locally
156: pnpm dev
157: 
158: # Run report UI locally
159: pnpm dev:report-ui
160: 
161: # Run report UI tests
162: pnpm test:report-ui
163: 
164: # Build report UI
165: pnpm build:report-ui
166: 
167: # Deploy latest worker
168: pnpm run deploy
169: 
170: # Set up worker infra
171: pnpm run setup:worker
172: ```
173: 
174: ## CLI Surface (Current)
175: 
176: ```bash
177: nimbus list
178: nimbus watch <job-id>
179: nimbus deploy checkpoint <checkpoint-id-or-commit-ish>
180: nimbus workspace create <checkpoint-id-or-commit-ish>
181: nimbus workspace show <workspace-id>
182: nimbus workspace deploy <workspace-id>
183: nimbus review create --workspace <workspace-id> --deployment <deployment-id>
184: nimbus review events <review-id>
185: nimbus review show <review-id>
186: nimbus review export <review-id> --format markdown --out <path>
187: ```
188: 
189: Important checkpoint flags:
190: 
191: - `--project-root <path>`: required for many monorepos
192: - `--no-dry-run`: actually creates/queues a live job
193: - `--no-tests`, `--no-lint`: skip validation steps in metadata
194: - `--env-file`, `--env KEY=VALUE`: pass environment inputs for preflight
195: 
196: Important workspace/review flags:
197: 
198: - `workspace deploy --auto-fix`: allow safe git baseline rehydrate remediation
199: - `workspace deploy --no-tests --no-build`: skip validation steps during manual cloud smoke flows

state.title: README.md

metadata.preview:
# Nimbus

Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.

This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.

Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)

## Current Focus

Nimbus currently supports a cloud review/deploy workflow built around immutable source snapshots:

- Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
- Create a workspace from that snapshot
- Run deploy validation and deployment inside Cloudflare Sandbox
- Persist replayable deploy/review lifecycle state in D1
- Generate non-mutating deployment-backed review reports

## What Works Today

parts.text: **Identifying stale recovery and timer leak bugs**

I found 2 issues in the changed code.

- **Medium – active reviews can be marked failed just by being viewed for >5 minutes**
  - In `packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:30` and `packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:53`, stale detection is capped with `REVIEW_STALE_RUNNING_MAX_MS = 240_000` plus a 60s grace, so any `running` review older than ~5 minutes is treated as timed out.
  - `handleGetReview` always calls this recovery path (`packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:725`), and the UI now polls every 3s while queued/running (`packages/report-ui/src/components/ReportPage.tsx:209`), so normal user viewing can repeatedly trigger timeout evaluation.
  - Realistic failure case: a legitimately long-running analysis (large context / slow provider) exceeds 5 minutes; a routine GET marks it `failed` with `review_execution_timeout` even though processing is still alive.
  - Suggested fix: do not hard-cap below `ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_MS` for read-path recovery, or gate recovery on additional liveness evidence (e.g., no new events for N minutes) rather than elapsed wall time alone.

- **Low – timeout fallback leaves dangling timers**
  - In the non-`AbortSignal.timeout` fallback, timers are created but never cleared in `packages/agent-endpoint/src/lib/agent.ts:157` and `packages/worker/src/lib/review-analysis.ts:701`.
  - Before this change, timers were explicitly cleaned up; now successful fast requests still leave a pending 90s/120s timer that later fires and aborts an already-completed controller.
  - This is mostly a resource leak on runtimes without `AbortSignal.timeout` support (or test environments forcing fallback), and can accumulate under load.
  - Suggested fix: keep a local `timeoutId` in the fallback and clear it in `finally`, or wrap fallback signal creation in a helper returning `{ signal, dispose }` and call `dispose()` after fetch completes.
